messages.subject: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Good Afternoon,

Below is the updated list of needed for you! 😊

  *   12 month lookback to show rents received for this property ( Usually the print outs from Airbnb, VRBO, or property management company work the best.)
  *   Most recent statement for the Hard Money loan against the property. Could you please also send us a good name, email address, and phone number for us to reach out and get a verification of mortgage form filled out?
  *   Most recent mortgage statement from Rocket Mortgage
  *   Fraud alert required to be unfrozen prior to final approval. Call 1-866-897-8216, File # L9610025B, and Reference Loan #110100031303
  *   Contact information for the private lien holder with the loan amount of $150,000 for 3917 E Elmwood St ( We will need a name, good email address an

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]
[

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]

messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Updated list of needed items! - Answered in blue



   - 12 month lookback to show rents received for this property ( Usually
   the print outs from Airbnb, VRBO, or property management company work the
   best.) - Attached is a 2025 Airbnb Report. We only listed it for a few
   months in 2025 to learn the ropes. We quickly became superhosts on airbnb
   and then put it on pause to begin additional construction projects in order
   to increase its amenities, beauty, and draw for future increased pricing.
   During those months we also rented the property out to a corporate retreat

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]
[

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]
[

messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]

messages.subject: RE: F3 Energy + Mood -Correct Formulation / Scale Formula Sheets
messages.body_text:
Harrison,

Now that you have quotes, the next step would be to put in a PO and provide timing on packaging and ingredients arrival timing. It sounds like 4-week lead time for Mood, so June production would be possible. For Energy Supplement products, we need to complete raw material testing once the product arrives, so production timing really depends on raw material arrival. Some of that testing could require 2-3 weeks, so we may be 6-7 weeks out, but once you have good dates for the arrivals and ingredients and packaging, we can provide better dates. Nothing happens on our side without a PO, so that would be the next steps. Sorry about the delay on the quote and the back and forth on the tolling vs turnkey quote. As we discussed, our team has been very busy with all of our new clients.

messages.body_text:
Harrison,

Now that you have quotes, the next step would be to put in a PO and provide timing on packaging and ingredients arrival timing. It sounds like 4-week lead time for Mood, so June production would be possible. For Energy Supplement products, we need to complete raw material testing once the product arrives, so production timing really depends on raw material arrival. Some of that testing could require 2-3 weeks, so we may be 6-7 weeks out, but once you have good dates for the arrivals and ingredients and packaging, we can provide better dates. Nothing happens on our side without a PO, so that would be the next steps. Sorry about the delay on the quote and the back and forth on the tolling vs turnkey quote. As we discussed, our team has been very busy with all of our new clients.

messages.subject: RE: F3 Energy + Mood -Correct Formulation / Scale Formula Sheets
messages.body_text:
Harrison,

Now that you have quotes, the next step would be to put in a PO and provide timing on packaging and ingredients arrival timing. It sounds like 4-week lead time for Mood, so June production would be possible. For Energy Supplement products, we need to complete raw material testing once the product arrives, so production timing really depends on raw material arrival. Some of that testing could require 2-3 weeks, so we may be 6-7 weeks out, but once you have good dates for the arrivals and ingredients and packaging, we can provide better dates. Nothing happens on our side without a PO, so that would be the next steps. Sorry about the delay on the quote and the back and forth on the tolling vs turnkey quote. As we discussed, our team has been very busy with all of our new clients.

send it over too..it will help to see it. Thanks

Ed

messages.body_text:
Bad news, the appraiser who quoted us the fee paid got back to us that he
cant find any comparables to complete the appraisal and asked us to
assign the order to someone else..all other quotes we received before were
much higher...$2000-2500 so we are going to send it back out to quote on to
see the best price we can get... do you know if the other appraiser you all
are getting is doing a short term rent schedule 1007 too? if so, do you all
need one from our appraiser too? if you only need the appraisal that will
give us more options for the appraisals since a lot of appraisers dont do
STR 1007s. We will let you know the new fee as soon as we get more
quotes back. When you get the other report from the 1st appraisal, please
send it over too..it will help to see it. Thanks

Ed

messages.subject: RE: F3 Energy + Mood -Correct Formulation / Scale Formula Sheets
messages.body_text:
Harrison,

Now that you have quotes, the next step would be to put in a PO and provide timing on packaging and ingredients arrival timing. It sounds like 4-week lead time for Mood, so June production would be possible. For Energy Supplement products, we need to complete raw material testing once the product arrives, so production timing really depends on raw material arrival. Some of that testing could require 2-3 weeks, so we may be 6-7 weeks out, but once you have good dates for the arrivals and ingredients and packaging, we can provide better dates. Nothing happens on our side without a PO, so that would be the next steps. Sorry about the delay on the quote and the back and forth on the tolling vs turnkey quote. As we discussed, our team has been very busy with all of our new clients.

messages.body_text:
Harrison,

Now that you have quotes, the next step would be to put in a PO and provide timing on packaging and ingredients arrival timing. It sounds like 4-week lead time for Mood, so June production would be possible. For Energy Supplement products, we need to complete raw material testing once the product arrives, so production timing really depends on raw material arrival. Some of that testing could require 2-3 weeks, so we may be 6-7 weeks out, but once you have good dates for the arrivals and ingredients and packaging, we can provide better dates. Nothing happens on our side without a PO, so that would be the next steps. Sorry about the delay on the quote and the back and forth on the tolling vs turnkey quote. As we discussed, our team has been very busy with all of our new clients.

messages.body_text:
Harrison,

Now that you have quotes, the next step would be to put in a PO and provide timing on packaging and ingredients arrival timing. It sounds like 4-week lead time for Mood, so June production would be possible. For Energy Supplement products, we need to complete raw material testing once the product arrives, so production timing really depends on raw material arrival. Some of that testing could require 2-3 weeks, so we may be 6-7 weeks out, but once you have good dates for the arrivals and ingredients and packaging, we can provide better dates. Nothing happens on our side without a PO, so that would be the next steps. Sorry about the delay on the quote and the back and forth on the tolling vs turnkey quote. As we discussed, our team has been very busy with all of our new clients.

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]
[

messages.subject: Re: Needed Items- Rogers
messages.body_text:
Also,

I wanted to touch base on the rates! Each time we got responses after locking hours and rates have opened up worse the very next day! Finally they have started to make some progress back,  7% today would be a cost of .324 points. Do you want me to lock it or keep floating to see if it can continue to keep getting a little better? Otherwise 7.125% would be no cost today!

If you want me to keep floating, you could also just let me know what the max points would be that you want to pay, and max rate. Then that way if I see the market turn for the worse, I know I can lock if its within those parameters. I know you are busy most of the day, so the later responses are totally normal! I just don’t want it to get to a point where you aren’t liking the rate at all!



[Zack Cole]
[
